prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene was performed using GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) in the gas phase. The structure is neutral (charge 0, multiplicity 1), with partial atomic charges computed via Mulliken analysis at the GFN2-xTB level. Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) were computed by finite differences of the electronic structure between the cation (charge +1, doublet) and anion (charge −1, doublet) states. All convergence checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f−) = 1.0000, confirming proper charge conservation. The iron center is the dominant reactive site: f⁺ = 0.22885 (most electrophilic), f⁻ = 0.25938 (most nucleophilic), with a small negative dual descriptor (−0.0305) indicating marginally nucleophilic preference. Cyclopentadienyl carbons show f⁺/f⁻ ≈ 0.0136/0.0122, and hydrogens f⁺/f⁻ ≈ 0.0635/0.0619, establishing iron as the primary reaction center. Mulliken charges show the iron as oxidation-state-like +0.42 e, while ligand atoms carry small negative (C, −0.077 e) and small positive (H, +0.035 e) charges. Integrity check status: ok / trustworthy.
